revision.text:
'''若宮八幡神社'''（わかみやはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]ある神社である。[[社格]]は旧県社。
==祭神== *大鷦鷯命・莵道稚郎子・宇礼姫・久礼姫
==歴史== この神社は、[[985年]]（[[寛和]]元年）紀兼貞によって創建されたと伝えられる。
==文化財== *大分県指定選択重要無形文化財 **杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭 *大分県指定天然記念物 **杵築若宮八幡社の境内林 など
==所在地== *大分県杵築市大字宮司336
==外部リンク== *[http://homepage1.nifty.com/kida/ 若宮八幡社サイト] *[http://bunka.oita-ed.jp/index.htm 大分県教育庁文化課]
{{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしや}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:杵築市]]

==外部リンク== *[http://homepage1.nifty.com/kida/ 若宮八幡社サイト] *[http://bunka.oita-ed.jp/index.htm 大分県教育庁文化課]

{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |画像 = [[画像:Gazoubosyu.jpg]] |所在地 = 大分県杵築市大字宮司336 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|25|41.4|N|131|36|36.8|E|scale:2000}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯命・莵道稚郎子・宇礼姫・久礼姫 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝寛和元年（985年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 12月1日 |神事 = }}
'''若宮八幡社'''（わかみやはちまんしゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[社格]]は[[旧県社]]。
== 祭神 == [[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命（おおささぎのみこと）]]、[[菟道稚郎子]]、'''宇礼姫'''（うれひめ）、'''久礼姫'''（くれひめ）を祀る。
== 歴史 == この神社は、[[寛和]]元年（[[985年]]）紀兼貞によって創建されたと伝えられる。
==文化財== *大分県指定重要無形民俗文化財 **若宮楽 **杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭 *大分県指定天然記念物 **杵築若宮八幡社の境内林
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 県選択重要無形文化財（杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭）]

{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=杵築市}} |所在地 = 大分県杵築市大字宮司336 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|25|41.4|N|131|36|36.8|E|scale:2000}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯命・莵道稚郎子・宇礼姫・久礼姫 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝寛和元年（985年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 12月1日 |神事 = }}
'''若宮八幡社'''（わかみやはちまんしゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]にある[[神社]]である<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社]</ref>。[[社格]]は[[旧県社]]。
== 祭神 == [[仁徳天皇|大鷦鷯命（おおささぎのみこと）]]、[[菟道稚郎子]]、'''宇礼姫'''（うれひめ）、'''久礼姫'''（くれひめ）を祀る。
== 歴史 == この神社は、[[寛和]]元年（[[985年]]）紀兼貞によって創建されたと伝えられる。
==文化財== *[[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]] **若宮楽 **杵築若宮八幡社の[[御田植祭]] *[[大分県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|大分県指定天然記念物]] **杵築若宮八幡社の境内林
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 県選択重要無形文化財（杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭）]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしや}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:杵築市の建築物]] [[Category:杵築市の歴史]]

{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=杵築市}} |所在地 = 大分県杵築市大字宮司336 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|25|41.4|N|131|36|36.8|E|scale:2000}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯命
莵道稚郎子
宇礼姫
久礼姫 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝寛和元年（985年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 12月上旬の土・日[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/event/index.html 祭典・行事予定] 若宮八幡社 |神事 = }} '''若宮八幡社'''（わかみやはちまんしゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社]。[[社格]]は[[旧県社]]。 == 祭神 == * 大鷦鷯命（おおさざきのみこと） - [[仁徳天皇]]。 * [[菟道稚郎子]]（うじのわきいらつこ） - [[応神天皇]]の皇子。大鷦鷯命（仁徳天皇）の異母弟。 * '''宇礼姫'''（うれひめ） * '''久礼姫'''（くれひめ） == 歴史 == この神社は、[[寛和]]元年（[[985年]]）12月に、勅宣により豊後国速見津郡八坂郷に下向した紀兼貞によって、[[石清水八幡宮]]に伝わる4柱の神像を守護し奉じて下司村柏島に創建されたと伝えられる。この神像は[[宇多天皇]]第9皇子の[[敦実親王]]が手ずから大鷦鷯命、菟道稚郎子、宇礼姫、久礼姫の像を白檀に奉彫したもので、[[延喜]]14年（[[918年]]）8月に[[仁和寺]]で[[開眼法要|開眼供養]]が行われ、石清水八幡宮に奉安されたものであった[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html 若宮八幡社について] 若宮八幡社。 その後、[[天喜]]5年（[[1057年]]）3月に生地村岳（現在の浜田社社地）、[[承安 (日本)|承安]]3年（[[1173年]]）9月に中村（現在の若八幡本社社地）に遷座した後、[[嘉暦]]元年（[[1326年]]）11月に現在の社地に遷座した。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）には県社に列せられている。 承安3年（1173年）に中村に遷した際、[[藤原伊通]]より裁許を得て豊後[[国司]][[難波頼輔|藤原頼輔]]監督の下で[[市場|市]]を開催した。これが[[放生会]]の神事と結びついて牛馬市として盛んになり、[[日本三大牛馬市]]に数えられるに至った{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf 若宮八幡社 社報 金鷹山 通巻第5号]}} 若宮八幡社社務所、2017年10月1日。牛馬の需要が減った戦後も、昭和25年（1950年）頃には約2千頭の牛馬が出されていた[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 昭和25年の記録 戦後20年の記録 アーカイブ] おんせん県おおいた!ちゃんねる（大分県）。 == 社殿 == === 摂社 === * 和漢将軍社 - 木付氏初代当主の[[木付親重]]を祭神とする[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html 境内のご案内] 若宮八幡社。 == 祭事・年中行事 == * [[歳旦祭]] - 1月1日 * 春季祖霊祭 - 春の[[彼岸]] * 春季大祭・[[御田植祭]] - [[4月6日]] * 仲秋祭・楽の市 - [[9月15日]]に近い日曜日 * 秋季祖霊祭 - 秋の彼岸 * [[新嘗祭]]・斗初穂 - [[11月23日]] * 例大祭 - 12月上旬の土・日曜日 == 文化財 == * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]] ** 若宮楽 - 1966年（昭和41年）3月22日指定。秋季祖霊祭に奉納される。 ** 杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭 - 2000年（平成12年）3月24日指定。 * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|大分県指定天然記念物]] ** 杵築若宮八幡社の境内林 - 1980年（昭和55年）4月8日指定。 * 杵築市指定有形文化財 ** 若宮八幡社大額 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社棟札 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社の石造物群 - 「寛永二十年」銘の鳥居等。1999年（平成11年）5月10日指定[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html 杵築地域 指定文化財一覧] 杵築市。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 県選択重要無形文化財（杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしや}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:杵築市の建築物]] [[Category:杵築市の歴史]]

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} * Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> * Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} * Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} * Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> * Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} * Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. * Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>

In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
