revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Deprecated qq open]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.

These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>

When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|12=draft}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=draft}} under the old Shinto shrine ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和|12=draft}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=draft}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|12=draft}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=draft}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|12=draft}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|12=draft}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜|12=draft}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=draft}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦|12=draft}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=draft}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=draft}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=draft}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会|12=draft}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=draft}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=draft}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=draft}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸|12=draft}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=draft}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=draft}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=draft}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)}} <!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)-->{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)}} <!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)-->{{citation needed|Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)}}

20 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市) up to rev 87584262
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=杵築市}} |所在地 = 大分県杵築市大字宮司336 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|25|41.4|N|131|36|36.8|E|scale:2000}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯命
莵道稚郎子
宇礼姫
久礼姫 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝寛和元年（985年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 12月上旬の土・日[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/event/index.html 祭典・行事予定] 若宮八幡社 |神事 = }} '''若宮八幡社'''（わかみやはちまんしゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社]。[[社格]]は[[旧県社]]。 == 祭神 == * 大鷦鷯命（おおさざきのみこと） - [[仁徳天皇]]。 * [[菟道稚郎子]]（うじのわきいらつこ） - [[応神天皇]]の皇子。大鷦鷯命（仁徳天皇）の異母弟。 * '''宇礼姫'''（うれひめ） * '''久礼姫'''（くれひめ） == 歴史 == この神社は、[[寛和]]元年（[[985年]]）12月に、勅宣により豊後国速見津郡八坂郷に下向した紀兼貞によって、[[石清水八幡宮]]に伝わる4柱の神像を守護し奉じて下司村柏島に創建されたと伝えられる。この神像は[[宇多天皇]]第9皇子の[[敦実親王]]が手ずから大鷦鷯命、菟道稚郎子、宇礼姫、久礼姫の像を白檀に奉彫したもので、[[延喜]]14年（[[918年]]）8月に[[仁和寺]]で[[開眼法要|開眼供養]]が行われ、石清水八幡宮に奉安されたものであった[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html 若宮八幡社について] 若宮八幡社。 その後、[[天喜]]5年（[[1057年]]）3月に生地村岳（現在の浜田社社地）、[[承安 (日本)|承安]]3年（[[1173年]]）9月に中村（現在の若八幡本社社地）に遷座した後、[[嘉暦]]元年（[[1326年]]）11月に現在の社地に遷座した。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）には県社に列せられている。 承安3年（1173年）に中村に遷した際、[[藤原伊通]]より裁許を得て豊後[[国司]][[難波頼輔|藤原頼輔]]監督の下で[[市場|市]]を開催した。これが[[放生会]]の神事と結びついて牛馬市として盛んになり、[[日本三大牛馬市]]に数えられるに至った{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf 若宮八幡社 社報 金鷹山 通巻第5号]}} 若宮八幡社社務所、2017年10月1日。牛馬の需要が減った戦後も、昭和25年（1950年）頃には約2千頭の牛馬が出されていた[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 昭和25年の記録 戦後20年の記録 アーカイブ] おんせん県おおいた!ちゃんねる（大分県）。 == 社殿 == === 摂社 === * 和漢将軍社 - 木付氏初代当主の[[木付親重]]を祭神とする[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html 境内のご案内] 若宮八幡社。 == 祭事・年中行事 == * [[歳旦祭]] - 1月1日 * 春季祖霊祭 - 春の[[彼岸]] * 春季大祭・[[御田植祭]] - [[4月6日]] * 仲秋祭・楽の市 - [[9月15日]]に近い日曜日 * 秋季祖霊祭 - 秋の彼岸 * [[新嘗祭]]・斗初穂 - [[11月23日]] * 例大祭 - 12月上旬の土・日曜日 == 文化財 == * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]] ** 若宮楽 - 1966年（昭和41年）3月22日指定。秋季祖霊祭に奉納される。 ** 杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭 - 2000年（平成12年）3月24日指定。 * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|大分県指定天然記念物]] ** 杵築若宮八幡社の境内林 - 1980年（昭和55年）4月8日指定。 * 杵築市指定有形文化財 ** 若宮八幡社大額 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社棟札 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社の石造物群 - 「寛永二十年」銘の鳥居等。1999年（平成11年）5月10日指定[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html 杵築地域 指定文化財一覧] 杵築市。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 県選択重要無形文化財（杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしや きつきし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:杵築市の建築物]] [[Category:杵築市の歴史]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

久礼姫 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝寛和元年（985年） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 12月上旬の土・日[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/event/index.html 祭典・行事予定] 若宮八幡社 |神事 = }} '''若宮八幡社'''（わかみやはちまんしゃ）は、[[大分県]][[杵築市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社]。[[社格]]は[[旧県社]]。 == 祭神 == * 大鷦鷯命（おおさざきのみこと） - [[仁徳天皇]]。 * [[菟道稚郎子]]（うじのわきいらつこ） - [[応神天皇]]の皇子。大鷦鷯命（仁徳天皇）の異母弟。 * '''宇礼姫'''（うれひめ） * '''久礼姫'''（くれひめ） == 歴史 == この神社は、[[寛和]]元年（[[985年]]）12月に、勅宣により豊後国速見津郡八坂郷に下向した紀兼貞によって、[[石清水八幡宮]]に伝わる4柱の神像を守護し奉じて下司村柏島に創建されたと伝えられる。この神像は[[宇多天皇]]第9皇子の[[敦実親王]]が手ずから大鷦鷯命、菟道稚郎子、宇礼姫、久礼姫の像を白檀に奉彫したもので、[[延喜]]14年（[[918年]]）8月に[[仁和寺]]で[[開眼法要|開眼供養]]が行われ、石清水八幡宮に奉安されたものであった[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html 若宮八幡社について] 若宮八幡社。 その後、[[天喜]]5年（[[1057年]]）3月に生地村岳（現在の浜田社社地）、[[承安 (日本)|承安]]3年（[[1173年]]）9月に中村（現在の若八幡本社社地）に遷座した後、[[嘉暦]]元年（[[1326年]]）11月に現在の社地に遷座した。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社、[[大正]]10年（[[1921年]]）には県社に列せられている。 承安3年（1173年）に中村に遷した際、[[藤原伊通]]より裁許を得て豊後[[国司]][[難波頼輔|藤原頼輔]]監督の下で[[市場|市]]を開催した。これが[[放生会]]の神事と結びついて牛馬市として盛んになり、[[日本三大牛馬市]]に数えられるに至った{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf 若宮八幡社 社報 金鷹山 通巻第5号]}} 若宮八幡社社務所、2017年10月1日。牛馬の需要が減った戦後も、昭和25年（1950年）頃には約2千頭の牛馬が出されていた[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 昭和25年の記録 戦後20年の記録 アーカイブ] おんせん県おおいた!ちゃんねる（大分県）。 == 社殿 == === 摂社 === * 和漢将軍社 - 木付氏初代当主の[[木付親重]]を祭神とする[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html 境内のご案内] 若宮八幡社。 == 祭事・年中行事 == * [[歳旦祭]] - 1月1日 * 春季祖霊祭 - 春の[[彼岸]] * 春季大祭・[[御田植祭]] - [[4月6日]] * 仲秋祭・楽の市 - [[9月15日]]に近い日曜日 * 秋季祖霊祭 - 秋の彼岸 * [[新嘗祭]]・斗初穂 - [[11月23日]] * 例大祭 - 12月上旬の土・日曜日 == 文化財 == * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]] ** 若宮楽 - 1966年（昭和41年）3月22日指定。秋季祖霊祭に奉納される。 ** 杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭 - 2000年（平成12年）3月24日指定。 * [[大分県指定文化財一覧#天然記念物|大分県指定天然記念物]] ** 杵築若宮八幡社の境内林 - 1980年（昭和55年）4月8日指定。 * 杵築市指定有形文化財 ** 若宮八幡社大額 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社棟札 - 1973年（昭和48年）4月13日指定。 ** 若宮八幡社の石造物群 - 「寛永二十年」銘の鳥居等。1999年（平成11年）5月10日指定[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html 杵築地域 指定文化財一覧] 杵築市。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ 若宮八幡社] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 県選択重要無形文化財（杵築若宮八幡社の御田植祭）] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしや きつきし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:杵築市の建築物]] [[Category:杵築市の歴史]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki|ja|杵築市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanwa|ja|寛和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanwa|qq=}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|qq=}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwashimizu Hachimangū|qq=}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|ja|豊後国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province|qq=}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|qq=}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|ja|仁和寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninna-ji|qq=}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tenki|ja|天喜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenki|qq=}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|qq=}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Kareki|ja|嘉暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kareki|qq=}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|qq=}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|qq=}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|qq=}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Hōjō-e|ja|放生会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōjō-e|qq=}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|qq=}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|qq=}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|qq=}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|qq=}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|ja|彼岸|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higan|qq=}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qq=}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|qq=}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|qq=}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|qq=}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|qq=}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|qq=}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>

== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Kitsuki
revision.text:
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|qq=}}, the ninth son of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]], from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during [[:en:Tengi|Tenki]] 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|qq=}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in [[:en:Karyaku|Kareki]] 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|qq=}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|qq=}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|qq=}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|qq=}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|qq=}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|qq=}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

<!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)--><!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)--> [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:杵築市の建築物]] [[Category:杵築市の歴史]] [[Category:杵築市の画像提供依頼]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|qq=}}, the ninth son of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]], from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during [[:en:Tengi|Tenki]] 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|qq=}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in [[:en:Karyaku|Kareki]] 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|qq=}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|qq=}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|qq=}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|qq=}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
