<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]], from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during [[:en:Tengi|Tenki]] 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in [[:en:Karyaku|Kareki]] 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}

== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

<!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)--><!--Wakamiya Hachiman-sha (Kitsuki, Ōita)--> [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kitsuki City]] [[Category:History of Kitsuki City]] [[Category:Image requests in Kitsuki City]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616257}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]], from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during [[:en:Tengi|Tenki]] 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in [[:en:Karyaku|Kareki]] 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>

In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Kitsuki, Ōita|Kitsuki]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]], Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Kanna (era)|Kanwa]] 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]], from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at [[:en:Ninna-ji|Ninna-ji]] in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during [[:en:Tengi|Tenki]] 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in [[:en:Karyaku|Kareki]] 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s [[:en:Life release|Hōjō-e]] ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}

[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]]

== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring [[:en:Higan|Higan]] * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616257]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya-hachimansha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (杵築市)|version=87584262|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]]

This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989}}}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

{{wikidata link|Q11616257}} [[Category:Buildings in Kitsuki City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Kitsuki City]] [[Category:Image requests in Kitsuki City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡社 (杵築市)]] {{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}}}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa|1=Kanna (era)}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki|1=Tengi}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki|1=Karyaku}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e|1=Life release}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Q24865773|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan|1=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/modules/itemmanager/index.php?like=0&level=4&genreid=8&itemid=160&storyid=1058 Intangible Cultural Heritage of Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]
{{shinto-stub}}

The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki|1=Tengi}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki|1=Karyaku}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e|1=Life release}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Q24865773|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan|1=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments; consolidate 4 interlanguage link(s) into wikidata link template [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24938264069|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|prefecture={{ill|Kitsuki|WD=Q850359|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.428167|N|131.610222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Nintoku|WD=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}; {{ill|Prince Uji no Wakiiratsuko|WD=Q11619989|1=Uji no Wakiiratsuko}}}} '''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kitsuki, Ōita|en|Kitsuki, Ōita|WD=Q850359|lt=Kitsuki|1=Kitsuki, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, Japan.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]</ref> The shrine held the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|旧県社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old Shinto shrine Ranking system.
== Deities Enshrined == * Ōsasazaki no Mikoto (大鷦鷯命) - {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. * Uji no Waki Iratsuko (菟道稚郎子) - A son of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} and half-brother to Emperor Nintoku. * '''Urehime''' (宇礼姫) * '''Kurehime''' (久礼姫)
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanna (era)|en|Kanna (era)|WD=Q1327053|lt=Kanwa|1=Kanna (era)}} 1 (985 CE) when {{ill|Ki no Kanesada|ja|紀兼貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kanesada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, under imperial order, brought four deity statues from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangū|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to a location in Kashiwajima, a village in the {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}. These statues were originally carved by {{ill|Prince Atsumi|ja|敦実親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Atsumi|1=Atsumi-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499521}}, the ninth son of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}}, from white sandalwood. The statues were consecrated at {{ill|Ninna-ji|en|Ninna-ji|WD=Q1202871|lt=Ninna-ji|1=Ninna-ji}} in 918 before being enshrined at Iwashimizu Hachimangū.<ref name="history">[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/history/index.html About Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
The shrine was relocated several times, first to its present site in Hamada during {{ill|Tengi|en|Tengi|WD=Q1193591|lt=Tenki|1=Tengi}} 5 (1057), then to Nakamura in {{ill|Jōan|ja|承安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōan|1=Shōan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11076350}} 3 (1173), and finally to its current location in {{ill|Karyaku|en|Karyaku|WD=Q1327198|lt=Kareki|1=Karyaku}} 1 (1326). In 1873, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gō-sha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gō-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and in 1921 it was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.<ref name="history" />
When the shrine moved to Nakamura in 1173, it was granted permission by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tomomichi|ja|藤原伊通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tomomichi|1=Fujiwara no Koremichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623024}} and oversaw by {{ill|Bungo Province Governor|ja|豊後国司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungo Province Governor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Namba Yoritsuke|ja|難波頼輔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Namba Yoritsuke|1=Nanba Yorisuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659573}}, to host a market. This event, later linked to the shrine’s {{ill|Life release|en|Life release|WD=Q11078912|lt=Hōjō-e|1=Life release}} ceremony, developed into a significant cattle and horse market, recognized as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|ja|日本三大牛馬市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Cattle and Horse Markets|1=Q24865773|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24865773}}.<ref>{{PDFlink|[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/kintakayama/shaho/kintakayama005.pdf Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Newsletter, Vol. 5]}} Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Office, October 1, 2017</ref> Even in 1950, around 2,000 livestock were sold annually.<ref>[http://www.onsenkenoita-ch.com/charms/detail/1286 Records from 1950] Ōita Prefecture Tourism Channel</ref>
== Shrine Buildings == === Sub-Shrines === * {{ill|Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja|和漢将軍社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakan Shōgun Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Dedicated to {{ill|Kitsuki Chikashige|ja|木付親重|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki Chikashige|1=Kitsuki Chikashige|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129199}}, the first head of the {{ill|Kitsuki clan|ja|木付氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitsuki clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://wakamiyahachimansha.com/precinct/index.html Grounds of Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine</ref>
== Festivals and Annual Events == * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} - January 1 * Spring Ancestral Festival - During the spring {{ill|Higan|en|Higan|WD=Q866977|lt=Higan|1=Higan}} * Spring Grand Festival & {{ill|Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Festival|lt=Otaue Matsuri|ja|御田植祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otaue Matsuri|qq=}} - April 6 * Mid-Autumn Festival & Market - Sunday near September 15 * Autumn Ancestral Festival - During the autumn Higan * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} - November 23 * Annual Festival - First weekend of December<ref name="event" />
== Cultural Properties == * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|大分県指定無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Wakamiya Dance (若宮楽) - Designated March 22, 1966; performed during the Autumn Ancestral Festival.<ref name="event" /> ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine's Otaue Festival - Designated March 24, 2000. * {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja|大分県指定天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Kitsuki Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Sacred Forest - Designated April 8, 1980. * Kitsuki City Designated Tangible Cultural Properties ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Great Plaque - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Ridge Tags - Designated April 13, 1973. ** Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine’s Stone Structures - Including a torii from 1643, designated May 10, 1999.<ref>[http://www.city.kitsuki.lg.jp/soshiki/24/kitukitiiki-siteibunnkazaiitiran.html Kitsuki Regional Cultural Properties List] Kitsuki City</ref>
